note: Toolbar Menu adds a custom menu (Structure > Menu) to the Drupal admin Toolbar as its own tab. Each tab is a `toolbar_menu_element` config entity (config prefix `toolbar_menu.toolbar_menu_element.<id>`) with fields id, label, menu, weight, rewrite_label, rendered by hook_toolbar(); managed at /admin/config/user-interface/toolbar-menu/elements (route entity.toolbar_menu_element.collection) under the `administer toolbar menu` permission. Cases span three tiers: easy/recipe (response-graded facts from 3.1.x/agent/), medium/introspection (a setup script installs a KNOWN toolbar_menu_element entity, the agent inspects the live site via drush to answer, a cleanup script removes it), and hard/execution (a reset script deletes matching elements, the agent creates a toolbar_menu_element linking a specific menu on the live site, a verify script checks live state — reset -> model -> verify). Grounded in module source (src/Entity/ToolbarMenuElement.php, config/schema, *.routing.yml).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Toolbar Menu (`toolbar_menu`) module, a menu I want in the admin toolbar is stored as which config entity type, and which fields identify the target menu and the tab ordering?
evals.expected_output: Each toolbar tab is a `toolbar_menu_element` config entity (config object toolbar_menu.toolbar_menu_element.<id>). The `menu` field holds the target menu's machine name and `weight` orders the tab; other fields are id, label, and rewrite_label.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site using the Toolbar Menu module, how does a custom menu (Structure > Menu) actually become a tab in the admin Toolbar? What renders it?
evals.expected_output: You create a `toolbar_menu_element` config entity referencing the menu; the module's hook_toolbar() implementation then renders one toolbar_item (a tab plus a tray) per element, ordered by weight, with the referenced menu's link tree pre-rendered into the tray.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Toolbar Menu module, at what admin path do you manage the toolbar menu elements, and which permission is required to reach it?
evals.expected_output: Manage elements at /admin/config/user-interface/toolbar-menu/elements (route entity.toolbar_menu_element.collection, the module's configure route), gated by the `administer toolbar menu` permission.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site has a Toolbar Menu element configured with the id `eval_tb_admin` (drush available). Inspect the running site and tell me which menu it puts into the toolbar and what its label is.
evals.expected_output: The `eval_tb_admin` toolbar_menu_element references the `admin` menu (Administration) and its label is `Admin Shortcuts` (read from its config: menu=admin, label='Admin Shortcuts').

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site a Toolbar Menu element with id `eval_tb_main` is configured (drush available). Inspect the running site and tell me whether it is set to display the underlying menu's own label instead of the element label (the rewrite_label setting), and which menu it targets.
evals.expected_output: The `eval_tb_main` element targets the `main` menu and has rewrite_label = TRUE, so the toolbar tab shows the menu's own label (Main navigation) instead of the element label.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), use the Toolbar Menu module to add the Main navigation menu (machine name `main`) to the admin toolbar as its own tab labeled `Site Menu`. Apply it to the live site as a toolbar_menu_element config entity; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: A `toolbar_menu_element` config entity exists with menu = main and label = Site Menu. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), use the Toolbar Menu module to add the Administration menu (machine name `admin`) to the admin toolbar as its own tab, and configure it to display the underlying menu's own label instead of the element label (rewrite_label enabled). Save it as a toolbar_menu_element config entity on the live site; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: A `toolbar_menu_element` config entity exists with menu = admin and rewrite_label = TRUE. Verified against live site state.
